GedcomX API¶

class gedcomtools.gedcomx.gedcomx.TypeCollection(item_type)[source]¶

Bases: Generic[T]

A typed, indexable, iterable container with small indexes on id/name/uri. The class name stays ‘Collection’; the element type is carried in item_type.

pop(index=-1)[source]¶

Pop and return an item at the given index (default: last), updating all secondary indexes.

Return type:

TypeVar(T)

by_id(id_)[source]¶

Return the item with the given id, or None if not found.

Return type:

Optional[TypeVar(T)]

by_uri(uri)[source]¶

Return the item whose URI matches, or None if not found.

Return type:

Optional[TypeVar(T)]

by_name(sname)[source]¶

Return items whose name matches sname (stripped), or None if not found.

Return type:

list[TypeVar(T)] | None

append(item)[source]¶

Append an item to the collection and update all secondary indexes.

Parameters:

item (TypeVar(T)) – The item to add; must be an instance of item_type.

Raises:

TypeError – If item is not an instance of item_type.

Return type:

None

extend(items)[source]¶

Append each item from an iterable to the collection.

Return type:

None

remove(item)[source]¶

Remove an item from the collection and update all secondary indexes.

Raises:

ValueError – If the item is not present in the collection.

Return type:

None

class gedcomtools.gedcomx.gedcomx.GedcomX(id=None, attribution=None, filepath=None, description=None, persons=None, relationships=None, sourceDescriptions=None, agents=None, places=None, events=None, documents=None)[source]¶

Bases: object

Main GedcomX Object representing a Genealogy. Stores collections of Top Level Gedcom-X Types. complies with GEDCOM X Conceptual Model V1 (http://gedcomx.org/conceptual-model/v1)

Parameters¶

idstr

Unique identifier for this Genealogy.

attributionAttribution Object

Attribution information for the Genealogy

filepathstr

Not Implimented.

descriptionstr

Description of the Genealogy: ex. ‘My Family Tree’

Raises¶

ValueError

If id is not a valid UUID.

version = 'http://gedcomx.org/conceptual-model/v1'¶
property contents¶

Return a dict with item counts for each top-level collection.

add(gedcomx_type_object)[source]¶

Dispatch a GedcomX top-level object to its appropriate add_* method.

Parameters:

gedcomx_type_object – A Document, Person, SourceDescription, Agent, PlaceDescription, Event, or Relationship instance.

Raises:

ValueError – If the object type is not a recognised top-level type.

add_source_description(sourceDescription)[source]¶

Add a SourceDescription to the genealogy.

Parameters:

sourceDescription (SourceDescription) – The SourceDescription to add; must have an id.

Raises:

ValueError – If the argument is not a SourceDescription or has no id.

add_document(document)[source]¶

Add a Document object to the Genealogy

Parameters:

document (Document) – Document Object

Returns:

None

Raises:

ValueError – If person is not of type Person.

add_person(person)[source]¶

Add a Person object to the Genealogy

Parameters:

person (Person) – Person Object

Returns:

None

Raises:

ValueError – If person is not of type Person.

add_relationship(relationship)[source]¶

Add a Relationship to the genealogy.

Also registers any embedded Person objects and updates the internal relationship table so each person can quickly retrieve its relationships.

Parameters:

relationship (Relationship) – The Relationship to add.

Raises:

ValueError – If the argument is not a valid Relationship.

add_place_description(placeDescription)[source]¶

Add a PlaceDescription to the genealogy.

add_agent(agent)[source]¶

Add an Agent to the genealogy, skipping duplicates by id.

Parameters:

agent (Agent) – The Agent to add.

Returns:

False if the agent’s id already exists; None otherwise.

Raises:

ValueError – If the argument is not an Agent.

add_event(event_to_add)[source]¶

Add an Event to this GedcomX genealogy.

Automatically assigns a uid if the event has no id. Duplicate events (by equality) are silently ignored.

Parameters:

event_to_add (Event) – The Event object to add.

Raises:

ValueError – If event_to_add is None or not an Event instance.

extend(gedcomx)[source]¶

Merge all top-level objects from another GedcomX instance into this one.

get_person_by_id(id)[source]¶

Return the first Person whose id matches, or None if not found.

source_by_id(id)[source]¶

Return the first SourceDescription whose id matches, or None if not found.

property id_index: Dict[Any, SourceDescription | Person | Relationship | Agent | Event | Document | PlaceDescription | Group]¶

Return a combined id→object mapping across all top-level collections.

property json: bytes¶

JSON Representation of the GedcomX Genealogy.

Returns:

JSON Representation of the GedcomX Genealogy in the GEDCOM X JSON Serialization Format

Return type:

str

class gedcomtools.gedcomx.person.Person(id=None, lang=None, sources=None, analysis=None, notes=None, confidence=None, attribution=None, extracted=None, evidence=None, media=None, identifiers=None, private=None, gender=None, names=None, facts=None, living=None)[source]¶

Bases: Extensible, Subject

A person in the system.

Parameters:

  • id (Optional[str]) – Unique identifier for this person.

  • name (str) – Full name.

  • birth (date) – Birth date (YYYY-MM-DD).

  • friends (List[Person], optional) – List of friends. Defaults to None.

Raises:

ValueError – If id is not a valid UUID.

identifier = 'http://gedcomx.org/v1/Person'¶
version = 'http://gedcomx.org/conceptual-model/v1'¶
add_fact(fact_to_add)[source]¶

Add a Fact to the person, skipping exact duplicates.

Return type:

bool

Returns:

True if the fact was added, False if it was a duplicate or invalid.

add_name(name_to_add)[source]¶

Add a Name to the person, skipping exact duplicates.

Return type:

bool

Returns:

True if the name was added, False if it was a duplicate or invalid.

display()[source]¶

Return a display summary dict with basic person information.

property name: str | None¶

Return the fullText of the first name form, or None if unavailable.

classmethod from_familysearch(pid, token, *, base_url=None)[source]¶

Fetch a single person by PID from FamilySearch and return a Person. - pid: e.g. “KPHP-4B4” - token: OAuth2 access token (Bearer) - base_url: override API base (defaults to settings.FS_API_BASE or prod)

property Narrative_for_Embedding: str¶
property ext_description: dict¶
class gedcomtools.gedcomx.person.QuickPerson(name: str, dob: str | None = None, dod: str | None = None)[source]¶

Bases: object

A GedcomX Person Data Type created with basic information.

Underlying GedcomX Types are created for you.

Parameters:

  • name (str) – Full name.

  • birth (date,Optional) – Birth date (YYYY-MM-DD).

  • death (date, Optional)

Raises:

ValueError – If id is not a valid UUID.

Surname = 'http://gedcomx.org/Surname'¶
property description¶
class gedcomtools.gedcomx.name.NamePart(type=None, value=None, qualifiers=None)[source]¶

Bases: object

Used to model a portion of a full name

including the terms that make up that portion. Some name parts may have qualifiers to provide additional semantic meaning to the name part (e.g., “given name” or “surname”).

Parameters:

  • type (Optional[NamePartType]) – Classification of this component of the name (e.g., Given, Surname, Prefix, Suffix, Title, Particle).

  • value (Optional[str]) – The textual value for this part, without surrounding punctuation (e.g., "John", "van", "III").

  • qualifiers (Optional[List[NamePartQualifier]]) – Optional qualifiers that refine the meaning or usage of this part (e.g., language/script variants, initials).

Examples

>>> from gedcomx.Name import *
>>> typ = NamePartType.Given
>>> given = "Moses"
>>> q = NamePartQualifier.Primary
>>> name = NamePart(type=typ,value=given,qualifiers=[q])
>>> print(name)
NamePart(type=Given, value='Moses', qualifiers=1)
identifier = 'http://gedcomx.org/v1/NamePart'¶
version = 'http://gedcomx.org/conceptual-model/v1'¶
class gedcomtools.gedcomx.name.NameForm(lang=None, fullText=None, parts=None)[source]¶

Bases: object

A representation of a name (a “name form”)

within a given cultural context, such as a given language and script. As names are captured (both in records or in applications), the terms in the name are sometimes classified by type. For example, a certificate of death might prompt for “given name(s)” and “surname”. The parts list can be used to represent the terms in the name that have been classified.

Parameters:

  • lang (Optional[str]) – BCP-47 language tag for this name form (e.g., “en”).

  • fullText (Optional[str]) – The full, unparsed name string as written in the source. If provided, the name SHOULD be rendered as it would normally be spoken in the applicable cultural context.

  • parts (Optional[List[NamePart]]) – Ordered structured components of the name (e.g., Given, Surname). If provided, fullText may be omitted. If provided, the list SHOULD be ordered such that the parts are in the order they would normally be spoken in the applicable cultural context.

identifier = 'http://gedcomx.org/v1/NameForm'¶
version = 'http://gedcomx.org/conceptual-model/v1'¶
class gedcomtools.gedcomx.name.Name(id=None, lang=None, sources=None, analysis=None, notes=None, confidence=None, attribution=None, type=None, nameForms=None, date=None)[source]¶

Bases: Conclusion

Defines a name of a person.

A Name is intended to represent a single variant of a person’s name. This means that nicknames, spelling variations, or other names (often distinguishable by a name type) should be modeled with separate instances of Name.

Advanced details

The name forms of a name contain alternate representations of the name. A Name MUST contain at least one name form, presumably a representation of the name that is considered proper and well formed in the person’s native, historical cultural context. Other name forms MAY be included, which can be used to represent this name in contexts where the native name form is not easily recognized and interpreted. Alternate forms are more likely in situations where conclusions are being analyzed across cultural context boundaries that have both language and writing script differences.

type¶

Classification of the name (e.g., BirthName, AlsoKnownAs).

Type:

Optional[NameType]

nameForms¶

One or more structured representations of the name (full text and parts).

Type:

List[NameForm]

date¶

Date context for this name (e.g., when the name was used or recorded).

Type:

Optional[Date]

identifier = 'http://gedcomx.org/v1/Name'¶
version = 'http://gedcomx.org/conceptual-model/v1'¶
static simple(text)[source]¶

Create a Name from a string, populating fullText and structured parts.

Return type:

Name

Supports GEDCOM slash notation where the surname is enclosed in slashes:

"John /Smith/" → Given=”John”, Surname=”Smith” "John /Smith/ Jr." → Given=”John”, Surname=”Smith”, Suffix=”Jr.” "/Smith/" → Surname=”Smith” only "John Robert /van der Berg/" → Given=”John Robert”, Surname=”van der Berg”

Without slashes the last whitespace-delimited token is treated as the surname when more than one token is present; a single token becomes a Given-name part.

class gedcomtools.gedcomx.agent.Agent(id=None, identifiers=None, names=None, homepage=None, openid=None, accounts=None, emails=None, phones=None, addresses=None, person=None, attribution=None)[source]¶

Bases: object

A GedcomX Agent Data Type.

Represents an agent entity such as a person, organization, or software responsible for creating or modifying genealogical data, as defined in the GedcomX conceptual model.

Static Methods:

Parameters:

  • id (Optional[str]) – A unique identifier for this agent. If not provided, one may be generated automatically using default_id_generator().

  • identifiers (Optional[IdentifierList]) – A list of alternate identifiers for this agent.

  • names (Optional[List[TextValue]]) – Names associated with the agent. Defaults to an empty list.

  • homepage (Optional[URI]) – A link to the agent’s homepage or primary website.

  • openid (Optional[URI]) – The OpenID identifier for the agent.

  • accounts (Optional[List[OnlineAccount]]) – Online accounts associated with the agent. Defaults to an empty list.

  • emails (Optional[List[URI]]) – Email addresses associated with the agent. Defaults to an empty list.

  • phones (Optional[List[URI]]) – Phone numbers associated with the agent. Defaults to an empty list.

  • addresses (Optional[List[Address]]) – Postal addresses associated with the agent. Defaults to an empty list.

  • person (Union[Resource, Person, None]) – A reference to the person represented by the agent. Accepts a Person object or a Resource reference. Declared as object to avoid circular imports.

  • attribution (Optional[object]) – Attribution information related to the agent.

  • uri (Resource, optional) – A URI reference for this agent.

add_address(address_to_add)[source]¶

Add an Address to the agent, skipping exact duplicates.

Raises:

ValueError – If the argument is not an Address instance.

add_name(name_to_add)[source]¶

Add a name (TextValue or str) to the agent, skipping duplicates.

Raises:

ValueError – If the argument is not a str or TextValue, or the value is empty.

add_note(note_to_add)[source]¶

Append a Note to the agent’s note list.

Raises:

ValueError – If the argument is not a Note instance.

add_identifier(identifier_to_add)[source]¶

Append an Identifier to the agent’s identifier list.

shares_name(other)[source]¶

Return True if this agent and other share at least one name value.

Return type:

bool

class gedcomtools.gedcomx.source_description.ResourceType(*values)[source]¶

Bases: Enum

Collection = 'http://gedcomx.org/Collection'¶
PhysicalArtifact = 'http://gedcomx.org/PhysicalArtifact'¶
DigitalArtifact = 'http://gedcomx.org/DigitalArtifact'¶
Record = 'http://gedcomx.org/Record'¶
Person = 'http://gedcomx.org/Person'¶
property description¶
class gedcomtools.gedcomx.source_description.SourceDescription(id=None, resourceType=None, citations=None, mediaType=None, about=None, mediator=None, publisher=None, authors=None, sources=None, analysis=None, componentOf=None, titles=None, notes=None, attribution=None, rights=None, coverage=None, descriptions=None, identifiers=None, created=None, modified=None, published=None, repository=None)[source]¶

Bases: object

Description of a genealogical information source.

See: http://gedcomx.org/v1/SourceDescription

Parameters:

  • id (Optional[str]) – Unique identifier for this SourceDescription.

  • resourceType (Optional[ResourceType]) – Type/category of the resource being described (e.g., digital artifact, physical artifact).

  • citations (Optional[List[SourceCitation]]) – Citations that reference or justify this source description.

  • mediaType (Optional[str]) – IANA media (MIME) type of the resource (e.g., "application/pdf").

  • about (Optional[URI]) – Canonical URI that the description is about.

  • mediator (Union[Resource, Agent, None]) – The mediator resource (if any) involved in providing access to the source.

  • publisher (Union[Resource, Agent, None]) – Publisher of the resource.

  • authors (Optional[List[Resource]]) – Authors/creators of the resource.

  • sources (Optional[List[SourceReference]]) – Other sources this description derives from or references.

  • analysis (Union[Resource, Document, None]) – Analysis document associated with the resource (often a Document; kept generic to avoid circular imports).

  • componentOf (Optional[SourceReference]) – Reference to a parent/containing source (this is a component/child of that source).

  • titles (Optional[List[TextValue]]) – One or more titles for the resource.

  • notes (Optional[List[Note]]) – Human-authored notes about the resource.

  • attribution (Optional[Attribution]) – Attribution metadata for who supplied or curated this description.

  • rights (Optional[List[Resource]]) – Rights statements or licenses.

  • coverage (Optional[List[Coverage]]) – Spatial/temporal coverage of the source’s content.

  • descriptions (Optional[List[TextValue]]) – Short textual summaries or descriptions.

  • identifiers (Optional[IdentifierList]) – Alternative identifiers for the resource (DOI, ARK, call numbers, etc.).

  • created (Optional[Date]) – Creation date of the resource.

  • modified (Optional[Date]) – Last modified date of the resource.

  • published (Optional[Date]) – Publication/release date of the resource.

  • repository (Union[Resource, Agent, None]) – Repository or agency that holds the resource.

  • max_note_count (int) – Maximum number of notes to retain/emit. Defaults to 20.

Raises:

ValueError – If id is not a valid UUID.

identifier¶

Gedcom-X specification identifier for this type.

Type:

str

identifier = 'http://gedcomx.org/v1/SourceDescription'¶
version = 'http://gedcomx.org/conceptual-model/v1'¶
property publisher: Resource | Agent | None¶

Return the publisher (Resource or Agent) for this source.

add_description(desccription_to_add)[source]¶

Add a TextValue description to the source, skipping duplicates.

add_identifier(identifier_to_add)[source]¶

Append an Identifier to the source’s identifier list.

add_note(note_to_add)[source]¶

Add a Note to the source, skipping duplicates and notes with no text.

add_source_reference(source_to_add)[source]¶

Add a SourceReference to the source, skipping duplicates.

add_title(title_to_add)[source]¶

Add a title (TextValue or str) to the source, skipping duplicates.

Raises:

ValueError – If the argument cannot be converted to a TextValue.

gedcomtools.gedcomx.date.date_to_timestamp(date_str, assume_utc_if_naive=True, print_definition=True)[source]¶

Convert a date string of various formats into a Unix timestamp.

A “timestamp” refers to an instance of time, including values for year, month, date, hour, minute, second, and timezone.

class gedcomtools.gedcomx.place_description.PlaceDescription(id=None, lang=None, sources=None, analysis=None, notes=None, confidence=None, attribution=None, extracted=None, evidence=None, media=None, identifiers=None, names=None, type=None, place=None, jurisdiction=None, latitude=None, longitude=None, temporalDescription=None, spatialDescription=None)[source]¶

Bases: Subject

PlaceDescription describes the details of a place in terms of its name and possibly its type, time period, and/or a geospatial description functioning as a description of a place as a snapshot in time.

Encapsulates textual names, geospatial coordinates, jurisdictional context, temporal coverage, and related resources (media, sources, evidence, etc.).

names¶

Human-readable names or labels for the place (e.g., “Boston, Suffolk, Massachusetts, United States”).

Type:

Optional[List[TextValue]]

type¶

A place type identifier (e.g., a URI). TODO: replace with an enumeration when finalized.

Type:

Optional[str]

place¶

Canonical identifier (URI) for the place.

Type:

Optional[URI]

jurisdiction¶

The governing or containing jurisdiction of this place (e.g., county for a town).

Type:

Optional[Resource|PlaceDescription]

latitude¶

Latitude in decimal degrees (WGS84).

Type:

Optional[float]

longitude¶

Longitude in decimal degrees (WGS84).

Type:

Optional[float]

temporalDescription¶

Temporal coverage/validity window for this description (e.g., when a jurisdictional boundary applied).

Type:

Optional[Date]

spatialDescription¶

A resource describing spatial geometry or a link to an external gazetteer/shape definition.

Type:

Optional[Resource]

identifier = 'http://gedcomx.org/v1/PlaceDescription'¶
version = 'http://gedcomx.org/conceptual-model/v1'¶
class gedcomtools.gedcomx.place_reference.PlaceReference(original=None, description=None)[source]¶

Bases: object

defines a reference to a PlaceDescription.

original¶

The unnormalized, user- or source-provided place text. Keep punctuation and ordering exactly as recorded in the source.

Type:

Optional[str]

description¶

A gedcomx.PlaceDescription Object or pointer to it. (URI/Resource)

Type:

Optional[Resource|PlaceDescription]

identifier = 'http://gedcomx.org/v1/PlaceReference'¶
version = 'http://gedcomx.org/conceptual-model/v1'¶
gedcomtools.gedcomx.identifier.make_uid(length=10, alphabet='abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyzABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZ0123456789')[source]¶

Generate a cryptographically secure alphanumeric UID.

Parameters:

  • length (int) – Number of characters to generate (must be > 0).

  • alphabet (str) – Characters to choose from (default: A-Za-z0-9).

Return type:

str

Returns:

A random string of length characters from alphabet.

class gedcomtools.gedcomx.conclusion.ConfidenceLevel(name, value)[source]¶

Bases: Qualifier

High = 'http://gedcomx.org/High'¶
Medium = 'http://gedcomx.org/Medium'¶
Low = 'http://gedcomx.org/Low'¶
classmethod from_json(data, context)[source]¶

Parse a confidence level from various input forms.

Accepts:

  • "High" | "Medium" | "Low"

  • "http://gedcomx.org/High" | ".../Medium" | ".../Low"

  • dict with keys type, value, confidence, level, or uri

  • existing ConfidenceLevel instance

Returns:

instance with .value set to the canonical URI.

Return type:

ConfidenceLevel

property description¶
class gedcomtools.gedcomx.conclusion.Conclusion(id=None, lang=None, sources=None, analysis=None, notes=None, confidence=None, attribution=None)[source]¶

Bases: object

Represents a conclusion in the GEDCOM X conceptual model. A conclusion is a genealogical assertion about a person, relationship, or event, derived from one or more sources, with optional supporting metadata such as confidence, attribution, and notes.

Parameters:

  • id (Optional[str]) – A unique identifier for the conclusion. If not provided, a UUID-based identifier will be automatically generated.

  • lang (Optional[str]) – The language code of the conclusion.

  • sources (Optional[List[SourceReference]]) – A list of source references that support the conclusion.

  • analysis (Union[Resource, Document, None]) – A reference to an analysis document or resource that supports the conclusion.

  • notes (Optional[List[Note]]) – A list of notes providing additional context. Defaults to an empty list.

  • confidence (Optional[ConfidenceLevel]) – The contributor’s confidence in the conclusion (High, Medium, or Low).

  • attribution (Optional[Attribution]) – Information about who contributed the conclusion and when.

  • uri (Resource, optional) – A URI reference for the conclusion. Defaults to a URI with the fragment set to the id.

  • links (_LinkList, optional) – A list of links associated with the conclusion. Defaults to an empty _LinkList.

identifier = 'http://gedcomx.org/v1/Conclusion'¶
version = 'http://gedcomx.org/conceptual-model/v1'¶
add_note(note)[source]¶

Add a Note to this conclusion, skipping exact duplicates.

Raises:

ValueError – If the argument is not a Note instance.

add_source_reference(source_to_add)[source]¶

Add a SourceReference to this conclusion, skipping exact duplicates.

Raises:

ValueError – If the argument is not a SourceReference instance.

class gedcomtools.gedcomx.qualifier.Qualifier(name, value)[source]¶

Bases: object

defines the data structure used to supply additional details, annotations, tags, or other qualifying data to a specific data element.

name str

The name of the Qualifier. It is RECOMMENDED that the qualifier name resolve to an element of a constrained vocabulary.

value¶

The value of the Qualifier. If provided, the name MAY give the semantic meaning of the value.

Type:

Optional[str]

identifier = 'http://gedcomx.org/v1/Qualifier'¶
version = 'http://gedcomx.org/conceptual-model/v1'¶
class gedcomtools.gedcomx.textvalue.TextValue(value=None, lang=None)[source]¶

Bases: object

identifier = 'http://gedcomx.org/v1/TextValue'¶
version = 'http://gedcomx.org/conceptual-model/v1'¶

Address data types for the GedcomX conceptual model.

This module contains classes used to represent postal addresses and related components in GEDCOMX documents. These classes follow the GedcomX v1 conceptual model specification.

Classes:

Address: Represents a structured postal address.

class gedcomtools.gedcomx.address.Address(value=None, city=None, country=None, postalCode=None, stateOrProvince=None, street=None, street2=None, street3=None, street4=None, street5=None, street6=None)[source]¶

Bases: object

GedcomX address data type.

Represents a postal address according to the GedcomX conceptual model.

Parameters:

  • value (Optional[str]) – A complete address as a single string.

  • city (Optional[str]) – Name of the city or town.

  • country (Optional[str]) – Name of the country.

  • postalCode (Optional[str]) – Postal or ZIP code.

  • stateOrProvince (Optional[str]) – Name of the state, province, or region.

  • street (Optional[str]) – First street address line.

  • street2 (Optional[str]) – Second street address line.

  • street3 (Optional[str]) – Third street address line.

  • street4 (Optional[str]) – Fourth street address line.

  • street5 (Optional[str]) – Fifth street address line.

  • street6 (Optional[str]) – Sixth street address line.

identifier¶

GedcomX identifier URI.

Type:

str

version¶

GedcomX conceptual model version URI.

Type:

str

Example

>>> addr = Address(
...     street="123 Main St",
...     city="Springfield",
...     stateOrProvince="IL",
...     postalCode="62704",
...     country="USA"
... )
>>> addr.city
'Springfield'
>>> addr.postalCode
'62704'
identifier = 'http://gedcomx.org/v1/Address'¶
version = 'http://gedcomx.org/conceptual-model/v1'¶
property value: str¶

class gedcomtools.gedcomx.serialization.Serialization[source]¶

Bases: object

static serialize(obj)[source]¶

Serialize a GedcomX object (or primitive) to a JSON-compatible dict/value.

Returns:

A JSON-serializable representation, or None if the input is None or empty.

classmethod apply_resource_resolutions(inst, resolver)[source]¶

Resolve any queued attribute setters stored on the instance.

Return type:

None

classmethod deserialize(data, class_type, *, resolver=None, queue_setters=True)[source]¶

Deserialize a JSON dict into an instance of class_type.

Parameters:

  • data (dict[str, Any]) – A JSON-compatible dict whose keys correspond to class_type fields.

  • class_type (type) – The target class to instantiate.

  • resolver (Optional[Callable[[Any], Any]]) – Optional callable to immediately resolve Resource/URI references.

  • queue_setters (bool) – If True, unresolved references are stored as lazy setters on the instance for deferred resolution.

Return type:

Any

Returns:

An instance of class_type populated from data.

classmethod resolve_references_recursive(root, resolver)[source]¶

Walk the graph rooted at root and resolve all gedcomx.Resource instances in-place (or by replacing container elements). - Handles dict/list/tuple/set - Uses SCHEMA to traverse fields on your model objects - Applies any queued _resource_setters - Avoids cycles and reuses a small cache so the same Resource isn’t resolved multiple times. Returns the (possibly same) root.

Return type:

Any

class gedcomtools.gedcomx.conversion.GxoObjectStack[source]¶

Bases: object

Level-indexed object stack (GEDCOM ‘level’ -> current GX object at that level)

This replaces the old push/pop hybrid behavior with what your converter actually uses everywhere: self.object_map[level] = obj and self.object_map[level-1].

get(level, default=None)[source]¶

Return the object at the given level, or default if not present.

Return type:

Any

items()[source]¶

Return (level, object) pairs for all entries in the stack.

clear()[source]¶

Remove all entries from the stack.

Return type:

None

parent(record)[source]¶

Return the object at the level directly above the given record.

Return type:

Any

ancestor(record, up)[source]¶

Return the object up levels above the given record.

Return type:

Any

set_level(level, value)[source]¶

Set the object at a given level and return it.

Return type:

Any

items_desc()[source]¶

Return (level, object) pairs sorted from highest level to lowest.

Return type:

List[tuple[int, Any]]

class gedcomtools.gedcomx.conversion.GedcomConverter[source]¶

Bases: object

type_name_type = {'aka': NameType.AlsoKnownAs}¶
personal_events = {'BARM', 'BASM', 'BLES', 'CENS', 'CHRA', 'CONF', 'CREM', 'EMIG', 'GRAD', 'NATU', 'ORDN', 'RETI', 'WILL'}¶
property ignored_tags¶

Return a dict of unhandled tag counts, or None if all tags were handled.

static clean_str(text)[source]¶

Strip whitespace and remove HTML tags from a string; returns ‘’ for None.

Return type:

str

parse_gedcom5x_record(record)[source]¶

Dispatch a single GEDCOM Element to its registered handler and recurse into subrecords.

Parameters:

record (Element) – The GEDCOM Element to process.

Raises:

  • AssertionError – If record is None.

  • ConversionErrorDump – On unhandled exceptions during conversion.

Return type:

None

convert_exception_dump(record, note=None)[source]¶

Library-safe dump: logs full stack context, then raises ConversionErrorDump. No prints.

Return type:

None

static format_counts_table(counts)[source]¶

Format a counts mapping as a multi-column text table sorted by count descending.

Return type:

str

static has_duplicates(seq)[source]¶

Return True if the sequence contains any duplicate values.

Return type:

bool

static find_duplicates(seq)[source]¶

Return a list of values that appear more than once in seq, preserving order.

unique(seq, key=None)[source]¶

Return a deduplicated list preserving first-occurrence order.

Parameters:

  • seq (Iterable[TypeVar(T)]) – Input iterable.

  • key (Optional[Callable[[TypeVar(T)], TypeVar(K, bound= Hashable)]]) – Optional callable to extract a comparison key from each element.

Return type:

List[TypeVar(T)]

Gedcom5x_GedcomX(gedcom5x)[source]¶

Convert a parsed Gedcom5x object to a GedcomX genealogy.

Primes id maps from GEDCOM cross-references, then walks every top-level record through the tag dispatch table to populate the GedcomX object.

Parameters:

gedcom5x (Gedcom5x) – A fully parsed Gedcom5x instance.

Return type:

GedcomX

Returns:

The populated GedcomX genealogy.

Raises:

ValueError – If gedcom5x is falsy.

handle_marr(record)[source]¶
if isinstance(self.object_map[record.level-1], Person):

gxobject = Fact(type=FactType.Marriage) self.object_map[record.level-1].add_fact(gxobject)

self.object_map[record.level] = gxobject

class gedcomtools.gedcomx.schemas.Schema[source]¶

Bases: object

Central registry of fields for classes.

  • field_type_table: {“ClassName”: {“field”: <type or type-string>}}

  • URI/Resource preference in unions: URI > Resource > first-declared

  • Optional/None is stripped

  • Containers are preserved; their inner args are normalized recursively

set_uri_class(cls)[source]¶
Return type:

None

set_resource_class(cls)[source]¶
Return type:

None

register_class(cls, *, mapping=None, include_bases=True, use_annotations=True, use_init=True, overwrite=False, ignore=None, toplevel=False, toplevel_meta=None)[source]¶

Introspect and register fields for a class.

  • reads class __annotations__ (preferred) or __init__ annotations

  • merges base classes (MRO) if include_bases=True

  • applies mapping overrides last

  • normalizes each type:

    strip Optional → prefer URI/Resource → collapse union to single

Return type:

None

register_extra(cls, name, typ, *, overwrite=False)[source]¶

Register a single extra field (normalized) and propagate to subclasses.

Return type:

None

normalize_all()[source]¶

Re-run normalization across all registered fields.

Return type:

None

get_class_fields(type_name)[source]¶
Return type:

Optional[Dict[str, Any]]

set_toplevel(cls, *, meta=None)[source]¶
Return type:

None

is_toplevel(cls_or_name)[source]¶
Return type:

bool

is_toplevel_obj(obj)[source]¶

Like is_toplevel, but takes an arbitrary object and checks its class.

Return type:

bool

get_toplevel()[source]¶
Return type:

Dict[str, Dict[str, Any]]

get_extras(cls_or_name)[source]¶

Direct extras declared on this class (not inherited).

Return type:

Dict[str, Any]

get_all_extras(cls_or_name)[source]¶

Direct + inherited extras for this class.

Return type:

Dict[str, Any]

property json: dict[str, dict[str, str]]¶
gedcomtools.gedcomx.schemas.type_repr(tp)[source]¶
Return type:

str

gedcomtools.gedcomx.schemas.schema_to_jsonable(schema)[source]¶
Return type:

dict[str, dict[str, str]]

gedcomtools.gedcomx.schemas.schema_class(mapping=None, *, include_bases=True, use_annotations=True, use_init=True, overwrite=False, ignore=None, toplevel=False, toplevel_meta=None)[source]¶

Decorator to register a class with SCHEMA at import time.

gedcomtools.gedcomx.schemas.bind_schema_property(cls, fget, *, schema, name=None, typ=None, fset=None, fdel=None, overwrite=False, doc=None)[source]¶

Bind module-level getter (and optional setter/deleter) as a property on cls, and register it in schema.

Return type:

property

gedcomtools.gedcomx.schemas.schema_property(*, schema, typ=None, name=None, overwrite=False)[source]¶

Use on a method inside a class.

Example

@schema_property(schema=SCHEMA) def display_name(self) -> str: …

gedcomtools.gedcomx.schemas.schema_property_plugin(*, name=None, typ=None)[source]¶
gedcomtools.gedcomx.schemas.apply_schema_property(cls, fget, *, schema, overwrite=False)[source]¶
gedcomtools.gedcomx.schemas.accept_extras(*, stash_attr='_extras', allow_only=None)[source]¶

Wraps the class __init__ to swallow unknown kwargs, and propagates to subclasses. - If a subclass defines its own __init__, it gets wrapped automatically via __init_subclass__. - Unknown kwargs are attached as attributes, or stashed in stash_attr if __slots__ present. - If allow_only is provided (callable(cls) -> set[str]), only those keys are accepted.

gedcomtools.gedcomx.schemas.extensible(*, allow_only=None, **schema_kwargs)[source]¶

Apply schema_class then accept_extras in the right order. Example: @extensible(toplevel=True)

gedcomtools.gedcomx.schemas.extensible_dataclass(*d_args, allow_only=None, **schema_kwargs)[source]¶
class gedcomtools.gedcomx.schemas.ExtrasAwareMeta[source]¶

Bases: type

Metaclass that intercepts cls(*args, **kwargs) and ensures registered extras are accepted.

  • Only passes constructor-known kwargs into __init__.

  • Allowed extras (from SCHEMA) are attached after construction.

gedcomtools.gedcomx.schemas.fact_from_even_tag(even_value)[source]¶
gedcomtools.gedcomx.schemas.event_from_even_tag(even_value)[source]¶
class gedcomtools.gedcomx.extensible.Extensible(*args, **kwargs)[source]¶

Bases: object

extras: Dict[str, Any]¶
classmethod define_ext(name, *, typ=None, default=None, overwrite=False)[source]¶

Declare an extra field on the CLASS.

Parameters:

  • name (str) – field name

  • typ (type | None) – Python type (used to update schema registry)

  • default (Any) – default value for new instances

  • overwrite (bool) – if True, replaces existing definition

Return type:

None

classmethod declared_extras()[source]¶
Return type:

Dict[str, Any]

gedcomtools.gedcomx.extensible.import_plugins(base_package, *, subpackage='extensions', local_dir='./plugins', env_var='GEDCOMX_PLUGINS', recursive=False, root_package='gedcomtools')[source]¶
Return type:

dict

class gedcomtools.gedcomx.header.Header[source]¶

Bases: object

class gedcomtools.gedcomx.family.FamilyParser(gx)[source]¶

Bases: object

reset()[source]¶

Reset all family members and the couple relationship for the next FAM record.

add_source_reference(source_ref)[source]¶

Add a SourceReference to the marriage fact.

add_note(note)[source]¶

Add a Note to the marriage fact.

set_marr_date(record)[source]¶

Set the marriage date from a GEDCOM DATE element.

set_marr_plac(record)[source]¶

Set the marriage place from a GEDCOM PLAC element, creating a PlaceDescription if needed.

set_husband(husband)[source]¶

Assign the husband (person1) of the couple relationship and register it in the genealogy.

set_wife(wife)[source]¶

Assign the wife (person2) of the couple relationship and register it in the genealogy.

add_child(child)[source]¶

Create ParentChild relationships between the child and each known parent.

class gedcomtools.gedcomx.zip.GedcomHeaderField(key, value)[source]¶

Bases: object

class gedcomtools.gedcomx.zip.GedcomResource(path, headers=None)[source]¶

Bases: object

class gedcomtools.gedcomx.zip.GedcomManifest[source]¶

Bases: object

class gedcomtools.gedcomx.zip.GedcomZip(path=None)[source]¶

Bases: object

__init__(path=None)[source]¶

Initialize a zipfile.

If path is provided:

  • Ensure directory exists or can be created

  • Use that path as the zip location

  • If any error occurs, fall back to a safe temp file

If path is None:

  • Always create a zip in the system temp directory

Result:

self.path -> Path to the zip file self.zip -> zipfile.ZipFile instance (write mode)

add_object_as_resource(obj)[source]¶

If obj is a top-level schema object, serialize it and store it as JSON inside the zip.

Returns the internal archive name (arcname) on success, or None if the object is not a top-level type.

Return type:

str | None

close()[source]¶

Close the underlying zip file if it’s still open. Safe to call multiple times.

Return type:

None

exception gedcomtools.gedcomx.exceptions.GedcomXError[source]¶

Bases: Exception

Base for all app-specific errors.

exception gedcomtools.gedcomx.exceptions.GedcomXDateParseError[source]¶

Bases: ValueError

Raised when we cannot parse a date string into GEDCOM X Date format.
